Dianjiang, a small county tucked away in southwestern China’s Chongqing, was transformed into a bustling live music hub in April as fans flocked to a concert headlined by Mandopop stars, including Jeff Chang.
Such scenes are no longer unusual. Once the preserve of megacities like Beijing, Shanghai a

news.headline: ‘Golden week’ begins with bang in Hong Kong, but restaurants’ gains ‘limited’
news.summary:
Hong Kong recorded a 13 per cent year-on-year increase in mainland Chinese visitors on the first day of the “golden week” Labour Day holiday, but a lawmaker for the restaurant trade noted that tourists’ shift towards more cautious spending resulted in only “limited” gains for the businesses.

news.headline: Why are modern Chinese audiences shying away from dubbed films?
news.summary:
At the December premiere of the Chinese-dubbed 1957 Billy Wilder classic Witness for the Prosecution in a Shanghai cinema, dubbing artist Qiao Zhen voiced a more immediate mystery: “Do you still enjoy Chinese-dubbed films?”
When audience members, mainly nostalgia-seeking fans, responded with applaus

news.headline: Korean Air bans roosters on US-Philippine routes amid cockfighting outcry
news.summary:
<p>Animal rights advocates were crowing on Saturday after Korean Air banned transporting roosters on flights from the United States to the Philippines, which has a huge cockfighting industry.</p>

news.headline: Timmy the Stranded Whale Is Released After an Elaborate Rescue
news.summary: The humpback whale, stuck for weeks in shallow waters off Germany, was towed to the North Sea in a barge. The story captivated the country.

news.headline: Oscars bans AI actors and writers
news.summary: Actors and writers created by artificial intelligence may be able to write and appear in films, but they will not qualify for Oscars under new rules issued by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, which address the use of AI for the first time. The role of artificial intelligence was one

news.headline: Data theft: Teen arrested over hacking of French government website
news.summary: A French teen taken into temporary custody over suspected involvement in the hacking of a government website. The Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (ANTS) stores personal identification data used for ID cards, passports, and driver’s licenses. The teenager is accused of taking part in a data bre

news.headline: Rebuilding Gaza Strip: Labour Day is the last thing on Palestinians' minds
news.summary: In the Gazan city of Khan Younis, workers are still clearing rubble seven months after the ceasefire that followed two years of war. The work continues around the clock, but it brings in only a few euros a day, barely enough to support their families. Liza Kaminov has more.

news.headline: Trump told Congress that hostilities "have terminated"
news.summary: Friday marked the expiration of the 60-day deadline for Donald Trump to secure congressional authorization to continue US military action in Iran under the War Powers Resolution. However, the US president argued that the law was unconstitutional and said the conflict had already ended under a ceasef

news.headline: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
news.summary: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.
